nodes.label: P02_T001
nodes.identity_summary: A highly brand-loyal consumer with sensitive skin who prioritizes ingredient safety and Ayurvedic formulations over trends or social influence. She conducts thorough research before purchases, strongly prefers international brands for gentleness, and maintains consistent routines rather than experimenting with new products.

nodes.label: extreme-brand-loyalty-with-sensitive-skin-justification
nodes.description: Repurchases the same products consistently and resists switching even when presented with better deals or recommendations, citing sensitive skin needs as justification.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Very loyal
[REAL] Repurchases same product
[SYNTHETIC] I'm very brand loyal and won't switch just for convenience
[SYNTHETIC] I'm very loyal to my brands and won't switch based on trends
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely dismiss it immediately unless it comes from their current trusted brands, because brand loyalty overrides product benefits for them.

nodes.label: ingredients-first-decision-maker
nodes.description: Ranks ingredients as the most important factor when buying personal care products and actively checks ingredient lists before purchasing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Ingredients, Reviews, Brand, Price
[SYNTHETIC] Ingredients are my top priority when buying personal care products
[SYNTHETIC] A: 10 minutes, B: 8 minutes, C: 2 minutes, D: 8 minutes, E: 2 minutes. Ingredients matter most to me
[SYNTHETIC] I'd flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash ad, this person would likely ignore marketing claims and immediately look for detailed ingredient information, making purchase decisions based on ingredient safety rather than benefits.

nodes.label: ayurvedic-natural-product-strong-preference
nodes.description: Strongly believes in natural/organic/ayurvedic products and is willing to pay 20-40% premium for Ayurvedic formulations while showing zero interest in clinical claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 5/5. Strong believer in natural/organic/ayurvedic products. Chemicals break sensitive skin
[REAL] Strongly prefers Ayurvedic
[REAL] Ayurvedic: 20–40%; Clinical: 0%
[SYNTHETIC] I strongly prefer Ayurvedic products and would want to learn about their sourcing story
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a clinical body wash versus an Ayurvedic one, this person would likely choose the Ayurvedic option even at higher cost, because they fundamentally distrust chemical formulations.

nodes.label: international-brands-for-sensitive-skin-preference
nodes.description: Prefers international brands over Indian brands because they believe international brands are gentler on sensitive skin, with Indian brands being too harsh unless organic.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Prefers international brands (e.g., Baylis & Harding) — gentler on sensitive skin. Indian brands harsh unless organic
[SYNTHETIC] I prefer international brands for sensitive skin
[SYNTHETIC] European brands are gentler on my sensitive skin, so the extra ₹100 is worth it
[SYNTHETIC] International premium brands: 4, Ayurvedic/natural brands: 3, Legacy Indian brands: 2
nodes.predictive_value: When shown two identical body washes from an Indian versus international brand, this person would likely choose the international one even at higher cost, because they associate international brands with gentleness.

nodes.label: thorough-researcher-before-purchase
nodes.description: Conducts extensive research including reading reviews, checking ingredients, and watching detailed YouTube content before making any new product purchase.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] I research thoroughly before buying anyway
[SYNTHETIC] I never buy personal care products without checking reviews first
[SYNTHETIC] 15-minute deep dive with ingredients and pH testing. I want detailed analysis
[SYNTHETIC] I'd rather wait and make an informed decision than buy impulsively
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash launch, this person would likely spend significant time researching ingredients and reviews rather than making an immediate purchase, even with attractive offers.

nodes.label: scent-for-personal-wellbeing-not-others
nodes.description: Chooses body wash scents for personal feel-good experience rather than to impress others, with strong preference for lavender and floral fragrances.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Grooming is self-oriented; scent for personal well-being, not others' perception
[REAL] Lavender is refreshing; flower scents = natural feel-good vibe. Scent for internal well-being
[REAL] Floral, Fresh Citrus
[SYNTHETIC] My grooming is self-oriented, not for impressing others
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash marketed for attracting others or social appeal, this person would likely be uninterested, preferring products marketed for personal relaxation and wellbeing.

nodes.label: extremely-risk-averse-to-new-brands
nodes.description: Rates comfort with trying new brands as 1/5 and consistently avoids unknown brands even with good deals or recommendations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 1
[SYNTHETIC] I don't try new brands, especially when I can't trust the ingredients list on some random newer brand
[SYNTHETIC] I'm very brand loyal and risk-averse; personal recommendations from trusted people matter most
[SYNTHETIC] Unknown brands could irritate my sensitive skin, and free products often have catches
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new D2C body wash brand with great reviews and pricing, this person would likely wait for extensive validation or avoid it entirely, because new brands represent unacceptable risk.

nodes.label: trusted-human-sources-over-digital-influence
nodes.description: Trusts dermatologists and friends/family (4/5) while rating TV ads low (2/5) and showing skepticism toward influencer content and social media recommendations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] TV ads: 2; Influencers: 3; Dermatologists: 4; Friends/family: 4; Online reviews: 2
[SYNTHETIC] I trust friends and family more than ads
[SYNTHETIC] Dermatologist recommendations rank high in my trust sources
[SYNTHETIC] I prefer human recommendations over AI suggestions
nodes.predictive_value: When shown influencer-endorsed body wash content, this person would likely be skeptical and seek validation from dermatologists or trusted friends before considering purchase.

nodes.label: price-conscious-within-comfort-range
nodes.description: Typically spends ₹100-200 on body wash and actively seeks deals, but won't compromise on skin-suitable products for savings.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] I'd wait 5 days for the Amazon delivery at ₹199. I'll use Pears soap for a few days rather than pay ₹100 extra
[SYNTHETIC] ₹250 is my ideal price point
[SYNTHETIC] I'm naturally price-conscious
nodes.predictive_value: When shown premium body wash options above ₹300, this person would likely seek cheaper alternatives or wait for sales rather than pay full price, unless it's from a trusted brand.

nodes.label: anti-gimmick-practical-buyer
nodes.description: Dismisses marketing gimmicks, seasonal variants, packaging upgrades, and trendy features, focusing only on core product functionality.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] I don't pay extra for seasonal gimmicks
[SYNTHETIC] ₹549 vs ₹249 for the same product quality makes no sense — I care about ingredients, not gimmicks
[SYNTHETIC] Designer packaging doesn't improve the product's effect on my skin
[SYNTHETIC] Virtual features are gimmicky - I need to know how it actually feels on my skin
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash with innovative packaging or trendy features, this person would likely dismiss these as unnecessary and choose the basic version, because they view extras as marketing manipulation.

nodes.label: mood-dependent-bathing-routine
nodes.description: Describes relationship with bathing/grooming as mood-dependent and prefers simple routines without complicated steps.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Mood-dependent
[SYNTHETIC] My bathing routine is mood-dependent, and I don't want extra steps
[SYNTHETIC] I prefer the convenience of ready-to-use body wash, especially when I'm not in the mood for elaborate routines
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash products requiring multiple steps or preparation, this person would likely avoid them, preferring simple squeeze-and-use formats that work regardless of mood.

nodes.label: established-platform-preference-over-new-channels
nodes.description: Strongly prefers buying from Amazon/Nykaa over brand websites, WhatsApp commerce, or new platforms due to trust and return policies.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] I don't trust brand websites for purchases
[SYNTHETIC] WhatsApp commerce feels too informal and risky
[SYNTHETIC] I prefer established platforms with proper return policies
[SYNTHETIC] Amazon link. Better return policy and customer service if something goes wrong
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash available only on a brand's direct website, this person would likely wait for it to appear on Amazon or find alternatives, because they prioritize platform trust over product access.

nodes.label: fragrance-non-negotiable-for-wellbeing
nodes.description: Considers fragrance essential for body wash experience and would reject unscented options, preferring lavender and floral scents for feel-good vibes.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Lavender-mogra blend, sensitive-skin safe
[SYNTHETIC] Not try it — fragrance is non-negotiable in body wash
[SYNTHETIC] Unscented would make my shower experience incomplete
[REAL] Scent; gentle on skin
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an unscented body wash marketed for sensitive skin, this person would likely reject it despite the skin benefits, because fragrance is essential to their shower experience.

nodes.label: volume-over-high-ratings-trust-pattern
nodes.description: Trusts products with thousands of reviews and moderate ratings over products with few reviews and high ratings, suspecting fake reviews.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] More reviews give me confidence, and I trust volume over potentially fake high ratings
[SYNTHETIC] 85 reviews could easily be fake or biased, but 12,000 reviews give a realistic picture
[SYNTHETIC] Brand A with 4.2 stars and 15,000 reviews. More reviews give me confidence
nodes.predictive_value: When shown two body wash options with different review patterns, this person would likely choose the one with more total reviews even if ratings are lower, because they distrust small sample sizes.

nodes.label: sensitive-skin-drives-all-decisions
nodes.description: Uses sensitive skin as the primary filter for all product decisions, immediately stopping use of products that cause any irritation.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Skin sensitivity
[REAL] Hot water causes redness/bumps; gentle lotion first to calm skin
[SYNTHETIC] My sensitive skin can't handle products that cause dryness
[SYNTHETIC] With sensitive skin, I'd feel changes immediately
nodes.predictive_value: When shown any body wash with potentially irritating ingredients or unknown formulations, this person would likely avoid it entirely, because sensitive skin reactions override all other product benefits.

nodes.label: consistency-over-variety-seeker
nodes.description: Prefers using the same products repeatedly rather than trying new variants or seasonal options, valuing predictable results.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] I stick with what works for my skin
[SYNTHETIC] I value consistency and trust over excitement in personal care
[SYNTHETIC] All the same brand and variant. I repurchase the same product when it runs out
[SYNTHETIC] Limited editions are pointless if I can't get them again
nodes.predictive_value: When shown limited edition or new variants from trusted brands, this person would likely stick with their regular version, because they prioritize consistency over novelty.

nodes.label: dermatologist-validation-seeker
nodes.description: Highly values dermatologist recommendations and would prioritize expert medical advice over other sources when making skincare decisions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Dermatologists: 4
[SYNTHETIC] I trust dermatologists over curated collections
[SYNTHETIC] Dermatologist recommendations rank high in my trust sources
[SYNTHETIC] I'd need multiple expert opinions before switching from something that works
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash content featuring dermatologist endorsements, this person would likely pay close attention and consider it credible, because medical expertise carries significant weight in their decision-making.

nodes.label: social-media-skeptic-with-privacy-concerns
nodes.description: Shows strong skepticism toward social media marketing, finds personalized targeting creepy, and avoids engaging with brand social content.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] That level of social media stalking is invasive and would make me avoid the brand completely
[SYNTHETIC] I don't trust bestseller tags without reviews
[SYNTHETIC] I don't care about others' perception of my grooming products
[SYNTHETIC] I ignore and continue scrolling
nodes.predictive_value: When shown targeted social media ads for body wash, this person would likely ignore or actively avoid the brand, because they view social media marketing as manipulative and invasive.

nodes.label: waste-averse-finish-before-switching
nodes.description: Always finishes current products before trying new ones and avoids buying multiple products simultaneously to prevent waste.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Finish your current bottle first, then switch. Wastage annoys me
[SYNTHETIC] I only buy what I need when I need it
[SYNTHETIC] I enjoy it until it's over and go back to my regular brand
nodes.predictive_value: When shown bundle deals or 'try now' offers while they have existing stock, this person would likely wait until their current product is finished, because they prioritize avoiding waste over trying new products.

nodes.label: clinical-claims-skeptic-prefers-real-evidence
nodes.description: Dismisses clinical claims without supporting data and prefers real user experiences over brand-sponsored studies or certifications.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Empty clinical claims don't impress me, but detailed ingredient explanations show genuine expertise
[SYNTHETIC] I'm naturally skeptical of marketing claims
[SYNTHETIC] Real user experience with similar skin concerns is more trustworthy than brand clinical claims
[SYNTHETIC] I don't trust random associations I've never heard of
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash with clinical testing badges or certifications, this person would likely ignore these claims and look for detailed ingredient information or user reviews instead.

nodes.label: ayurvedic-formulation-preference
nodes.description: Consistently expresses desire for Ayurvedic ingredients over clinical formulations across multiple product concepts.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Better ingredients transparency and more Ayurvedic formulations for sensitive skin without harsh chemicals
[SURVEY] I'd prefer if it used more natural Ayurvedic ingredients instead of clinical formulations for the calming effects

[SURVEY] I'd want them to remove the AI photo upload requirement and instead use a detailed questionnaire about skin concerns, plus provide complete ingredient transparency and Ayurvedic formulation options
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new body wash concepts, this person would likely prefer products marketed with traditional/natural ingredients over modern chemical formulations.

nodes.label: complete-ingredient-transparency-seeker
nodes.description: Repeatedly demands to see complete ingredient lists before considering any new product, regardless of brand trust.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] I'd want to see the complete ingredient list and proof that it's safe for sensitive skin before considering it
[SURVEY] I'd need to see the ingredient list for the lipid-lock technology and ensure it's gentle enough for sensitive skin
[SURVEY] Better ingredients transparency and more Ayurvedic formulations for sensitive skin
nodes.predictive_value: When shown product concepts with proprietary technologies, this person would likely withhold purchase intent until full ingredient disclosure is provided.

nodes.label: personal-wellbeing-scent-selector
nodes.description: Chooses fragrances based on personal well-being benefits rather than social appeal or matching with others.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] The lavender oils appeal to me since I love lavender scents for personal well-being and relaxation
[SURVEY] Nothing appeals to me - I choose scents for my own well-being, not to match with someone else
[SURVEY] I prefer choosing scents based on my own well-being and skin needs rather than matching with someone else
nodes.predictive_value: When shown fragrance concepts, this person would likely respond positively to wellness-focused scent benefits but reject socially-oriented fragrance positioning.

nodes.label: technology-privacy-skeptic
nodes.description: Shows strong resistance to AI and data collection technologies, preferring traditional questionnaire approaches.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] The customization aspect sounds interesting in theory, but I'm concerned about the AI technology and data privacy involved in uploading skin photos
[SURVEY] I'd want them to remove the AI photo upload requirement and instead use a detailed questionnaire
nodes.predictive_value: When shown tech-enabled beauty concepts, this person would likely reject products requiring personal data upload but accept traditional customization methods.

nodes.label: moisturization-prioritizer
nodes.description: Rates moisturization as highest importance factor and shows strong appeal for moisturizing benefits in preferred concepts.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Moisturization / skin hydration: 5
[SURVEY] The moisturizing benefit appeals to me since I want moisturized skin after washing, and it would simplify my routine
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating body wash concepts, this person would likely prioritize and respond most positively to prominent moisturizing claims.

nodes.label: sensitive-skin-safety-first
nodes.description: Consistently expresses concern about skin irritation and demands gentleness proof across all new product considerations.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Could irritate my skin (selected for multiple products)
[SURVEY] I'd want to see the complete ingredient list and proof that it's safe for sensitive skin
[SURVEY] ensure it's gentle enough for sensitive skin
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new formulations, this person would likely require explicit sensitive skin safety claims and proof before considering purchase.

nodes.label: brand-expectation-alignment-critical
nodes.description: Shows clear preference for concepts that fit well with Dove brand expectations, rejecting those that don't align.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Dove Skip - Fits very well
[SURVEY] Dove Night Wash - Fits very well
[SURVEY] Dove 60-Second Body Spray - Doesn't fit very well
[SURVEY] Dove Skin ID - Doesn't fit very well
nodes.predictive_value: When shown brand extensions, this person would likely reject concepts that feel inconsistent with their established brand expectations, regardless of innovation level.

nodes.label: routine-simplification-appreciator
nodes.description: Values products that can simplify daily routines, particularly appealing to time-saving moisturization benefits.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] The moisturizing benefit appeals to me since I want moisturized skin after washing, and it would simplify my routine
[SURVEY] How likely would the Dove Skip be to save you time in your routine: 4
nodes.predictive_value: When shown multi-benefit concepts, this person would likely respond positively to products that eliminate separate routine steps while maintaining quality.

nodes.label: innovation-skeptical-traditionalist
nodes.description: Rates innovation as low importance and consistently selects 'Don't trust new products' as a concern across multiple concepts.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Innovation / uniqueness: 2
[SURVEY] Don't trust new products (selected for 60-Second Body Spray, Yours & Mine, and Skin ID)
[SURVEY] Brand trust and reputation: 5
nodes.predictive_value: When shown highly innovative concepts, this person would likely prefer established formulations and be skeptical of breakthrough technologies.

nodes.label: price-threshold-conscious
nodes.description: Consistently cites price concerns across all concepts but shows willingness to pay reasonable amounts for preferred products.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Price might be too high (selected for all 5 concepts)
[SURVEY] What would you expect to pay for your most preferred concept: 299
[SURVEY] Value for money: 4
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new concepts, this person would likely evaluate all products through a price lens but accept fair pricing for products meeting their core needs.

nodes.label: fragrance-quality-essential
nodes.description: Rates fragrance quality as maximum importance, equal to moisturization and gentleness priorities.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Fragrance / scent quality: 5
[SURVEY] The lavender oils appeal to me since I love lavender scents for personal well-being and relaxation
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating body wash options, this person would likely reject products with poor or artificial fragrances regardless of other benefits.

nodes.label: current-product-dissatisfaction
nodes.description: Expresses dissatisfaction with current body wash, indicating openness to switching for the right alternative.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Overall, how satisfied are you with the body wash or shower gel you currently use most often: Somewhat dissatisfied
nodes.predictive_value: When shown alternative products that address their core needs, this person would likely be more receptive to switching than satisfied consumers.

nodes.label: dove-lux-brand-loyalty
nodes.description: Uses only Dove and Lux brands, showing concentrated brand loyalty in personal care category.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Which body wash or shower gel brands have you used in the past 3 months: ['Dove', 'Lux']
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new brand concepts, this person would likely be most receptive to extensions from Dove or Lux rather than completely new brands.

nodes.label: concept-relevance-decisive
nodes.description: Shows strong correlation between personal relevance perception and purchase intent across all concepts.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Dove Skip - Very relevant, Probably would buy
[SURVEY] Dove Night Wash - Very relevant, Probably would buy
[SURVEY] Dove 60-Second Body Spray - Not very relevant, Probably would not buy
[SURVEY] Dove Skin ID - Not very relevant, Definitely would not buy
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating new concepts, this person's purchase intent would likely be primarily driven by how personally relevant they perceive the product benefits.

nodes.label: uniqueness-appreciation-without-adoption
nodes.description: Recognizes and appreciates product uniqueness but doesn't translate this into purchase intent for unfamiliar concepts.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Dove 60-Second Body Spray - Very unique, Probably would not buy
[SURVEY] Dove Skin ID - Extremely unique, Definitely would not buy
[SURVEY] Dove Yours & Mine - Very unique, Probably would not buy
nodes.predictive_value: When shown unique innovations, this person would likely acknowledge the novelty but require strong relevance and safety proof before considering purchase.

nodes.label: believability-threshold-moderate
nodes.description: Consistently rates product claims as 'somewhat believable' across concepts, showing measured skepticism rather than outright disbelief.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] How believable are the claims made about the Dove 60-Second Body Spray: Somewhat believable
[SURVEY] How believable are the claims made about the Dove Skip: Somewhat believable
[SURVEY] How believable are the claims made about the Dove Night Wash: Somewhat believable
nodes.predictive_value: When shown product claims, this person would likely maintain moderate skepticism requiring additional proof rather than immediate acceptance or rejection.

nodes.label: night-routine-wellness-oriented
nodes.description: Shows highest interest and appeal for nighttime wellness concepts, rating Night Wash as most preferred.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Dove Night Wash ranked #1 in preference
[SURVEY] The lavender oils appeal to me since I love lavender scents for personal well-being and relaxation
[SURVEY] How well does the Dove Night Wash concept fit with your current routine: 4
nodes.predictive_value: When shown time-specific product concepts, this person would likely respond most positively to evening/nighttime wellness-focused formulations.

nodes.label: convenience-secondary-to-quality
nodes.description: Rates convenience as moderate importance while prioritizing quality factors like gentleness and moisturization much higher.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Convenience / ease of use: 3
[SURVEY] Gentleness on skin: 5
[SURVEY] Moisturization / skin hydration: 5
[SURVEY] Brand trust and reputation: 5
nodes.predictive_value: When shown convenience-focused concepts, this person would likely prioritize product quality and skin benefits over time-saving features.

nodes.label: micellar-technology-cautious-interest
nodes.description: Shows specific interest in micellar technology for gentleness but maintains caution about unknown chemical components.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] The micellar technology sounds gentle, but I'm concerned about unknown chemicals and whether it's truly suitable for sensitive skin
nodes.predictive_value: When shown products with specific gentle technologies, this person would likely show initial interest but require detailed safety information before purchase consideration.

nodes.label: daily-usage-routine-established
nodes.description: Uses body wash daily, indicating established routine and regular product consumption patterns.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] How often do you personally use body wash or shower gel: Daily
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new products, this person would likely evaluate how well they integrate into an established daily washing routine rather than occasional use scenarios.

nodes.label: social-matching-concept-rejector
nodes.description: Explicitly rejects products designed for social coordination or matching with others, preferring individual choice.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Nothing appeals to me - I choose scents for my own well-being, not to match with someone else
[SURVEY] the entire concept doesn't appeal to me - I prefer choosing scents based on my own well-being and skin needs rather than matching with someone else
nodes.predictive_value: When shown couple-oriented or socially-coordinated product concepts, this person would likely reject them in favor of individually-focused alternatives.

nodes.label: questionnaire-over-technology-preference
nodes.description: Prefers traditional questionnaire-based customization over AI or photo-based technology solutions.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] I'd want them to remove the AI photo upload requirement and instead use a detailed questionnaire about skin concerns
nodes.predictive_value: When shown customization options, this person would likely prefer detailed form-based personalization over app-based or photo-analysis technologies.

nodes.label: premium-perception-quality-seeker
nodes.description: Rates preferred concepts as premium rather than basic, indicating association of quality with premium positioning.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Dove Skip Basic-Premium: 4
[SURVEY] Dove Night Wash Basic-Premium: 4
[SURVEY] Brand trust and reputation: 5
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating product positioning, this person would likely respond better to premium-positioned products that signal quality rather than value-focused messaging.

nodes.label: memorable-experience-seeker
nodes.description: Rates most preferred concepts as highly memorable, indicating desire for distinctive product experiences.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Dove Night Wash Forgettable-Memorable: 5
[SURVEY] Dove Skip Forgettable-Memorable: 4
nodes.predictive_value: When shown product concepts, this person would likely prefer products that offer distinctive, memorable experiences over routine functional benefits.

nodes.label: cleansing-effectiveness-baseline-expectation
nodes.description: Rates cleansing effectiveness as important but secondary to moisturization, gentleness, and fragrance quality.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Cleansing effectiveness: 4
[SURVEY] Moisturization / skin hydration: 5
[SURVEY] Gentleness on skin: 5
[SURVEY] Fragrance / scent quality: 5
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating body wash concepts, this person would likely expect effective cleansing as a given but make decisions based on additional skin and sensory benefits.

links.mechanism: Brand loyalty naturally leads to preferring the same products repeatedly rather than seeking variety, because switching represents unnecessary risk.
Her focus on ingredients leads her to prefer natural/Ayurvedic formulations because she can understand and trust plant-based ingredients over synthetic chemicals.
Her fear of trying new brands drives extensive research behavior as a way to minimize risk before making any purchase decision.
Her preference for human recommendations over digital content creates natural skepticism toward social media marketing and influencer endorsements.
Being price-conscious makes her avoid waste by finishing products before buying new ones, maximizing value from each purchase.
Her dismissal of marketing gimmicks reinforces her focus on actual ingredients and formulations as the only factors that matter for product performance.
Having mood-dependent routines makes her prefer simple, straightforward products that work regardless of her energy level, avoiding complicated features.
Having sensitive skin creates fear of reactions, making her stick to proven products rather than risk trying new brands that might cause irritation.
Her sensitive skin experiences have taught her that international brands are gentler, creating a strong preference for these brands over Indian alternatives.
Her skepticism of marketing claims extends to review patterns, preferring large volumes of real user feedback over potentially manipulated high ratings.
Preference for traditional Ayurvedic ingredients drives the need to verify that products actually contain natural rather than synthetic components through full ingredient disclosure.
Focus on individual wellness benefits as the primary scent selection criteria directly conflicts with products designed for social coordination or matching with others.
High priority on moisturization makes products that combine cleansing and moisturizing particularly appealing as they address the top priority while simplifying routine.
Concern about skin irritation creates the fundamental need to examine all ingredients before trying any new product, making transparency a prerequisite for consideration.
Strong expectations for how trusted brands should behave reinforces loyalty to familiar brands like Dove and Lux while creating barriers to brand switching.
General distrust of new products extends specifically to technology-driven solutions, creating compounded resistance to AI and data collection features.
General skepticism toward new products allows recognition of innovation while preventing translation of uniqueness appreciation into actual purchase intent.
Dissatisfaction with current products creates openness to alternatives, but only for concepts that directly address personal needs and concerns.
Moderate skepticism about product claims creates the need for additional verification through ingredient transparency rather than accepting marketing promises at face value.
Prioritizing quality over convenience reinforces the need to ensure skin safety and gentleness rather than choosing products primarily for time-saving benefits.
